response.justification: Strong, broad software engineering background with deep open-source and Linux/Unix experience and a genuine track record of building and self-hosting systems (GISCollective, CI/CD, GitLab, Docker). However, the role is an executive-level leadership position and the resume shows individual-contributor and founder work rather than a clear record of leading and mentoring an interdisciplinary team or formal technical project management. Critically, the role requires German at minimum C1, while the candidate lists only Basic German, and there is no mention of UML modeling, requirements management, or IT security/compliance — all explicitly required. Worth a screening conversation to probe leadership scope and German proficiency, but not a clear fit on paper.
strengths: Extensive, versatile modern software development experience across web, backend, mobile, and embedded
Strong open-source and Linux/Unix orientation, including founding and operating an OSS Geo-Data platform
Proven craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, DevOps, teaching/workshops) signaling mentorship aptitude
concerns: German listed as Basic, well below the required minimum C1
No explicit team leadership, people management, or technical project management role
No mention of UML/modeling, requirements management, or IT security/risk/compliance

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal leadership/management track record
